observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct:
The MTO to prepare and maintain cashbooks for all the accounts kept by the LGU; and
The MTO and MACCO to immediately reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure accuracy of the cash accounts.

We recommended that management prioritize the determination of those unidentified PPE items, and make necessary adjustments whenever applicable, to accurately reflect the proper account balances of the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other for the appropriate transfer of the completed projects to the proper PPE accounts; and
Verify the existence and completeness of the projects and to make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the completed projects.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory accounts all purchases of materials and supplies upon acceptance of the delivered items. In compliance with the Construction Period Theory, record the corresponding CIP accounts only when the inventories of supplies and materials are used during construction. For other inventory accounts, recognize the expense upon consumption or distribution; and
Observe proper recording of transactions by referring to the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to provide a fair financial statement presentation.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the Municipal Accountant to demand from the NGOs/POs to submit the documentary requirements to facilitate the recording of the liquidation of funds transferred;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts for the liquidation of “Due from NGOs/POs” account to avoid dormancy and to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 for the request for write-off of dormant accounts; and
Exhaust all legal efforts to demand from the concerned NGOs/POs to produce and submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents of the utilized funds transferred to their respective organizations.

We recommended that management:
Enforce the immediate liquidation of the transferred funds to the different barangays by sending demand letters and to require the barangays to submit a status report on the project implementation if the project is still not completed; and
Execute a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for all succeeding transactions involving fund transfers to ensure that the LGU and the recipient agency are aware of their responsibilities.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to a) submit the required supporting documents, b) make sure that all supporting documents are complete for all disbursements made, and c) strictly observe the guidelines for each program implemented by the Municipality especially on the required documents to be complied with to preclude the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the MDC properly document in its Minutes of Meetings, the presentations and deliberations on the exclusion and inclusion of PPAs for inclusion in the AIP/Supplemental Investment Programs. We also recommended that the MDC explain in writing the reason for the exclusion of the said PPAs from the revised AIP.

We recommended that the Management to:
Thoroughly plan and ensure proper monitoring of the projects and activities to be implemented under the LDRRMF such as the preparation of a monitoring report that would check the status of each project/activity;
Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in disaster preparedness and mitigation; and
That the excess unused appropriation will be reprogrammed for other disaster preparedness and mitigation activities for inclusion in the revised Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2022.

We recommended that the Management:
For succeeding budget preparations, appropriately prepare a Revised Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in case there are changes in the line items in the budget for LDRRMF and secure the necessary authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for its approval and funding;
Observe proper recording of financial transactions by referring to the Chart of Accounts provided in Volume III of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs; and
Ensure strict adherence to the provisions to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office observe the proper recording of transactions in consonance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to provide a fair financial statement presentation.

We recommended that management submit immediately the lacking documents for us to make a decision in audit, and to strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of benefits, especially on the required documents to be complied with to preclude the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

Thoroughly plan and propose only PPAs for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed; and

Explain in writing the reason/s on the recurrence of such deficiencies for us to further evaluate whether to suspend or disallow the financial transactions involved.

Stop the practice of allowing the collecting officers of the Municipality to draw cash advance;

Stop also the practice of transferring cash advances drawn to other personnel; and

Consider designating personnel from the other office/department as a disbursing officer of the cash advances for special purpose/s.

We recommended that management strictly observe the deadline on the submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the cash advances drawn for Confidential Fund. Direct the personnel-in-charge of the preparation of the documents to be aware of the deadline that these should be submitted to COA ATL within seven (7) days after release of check.

We recommend that management:

Plan thoroughly the projects to be implemented and assist the beneficiaries in looking for prospective buyers of their planted seedlings; and

Strictly monitor the implementation of the projects and consider establishing a system for project monitoring, so that prompt action can be made to address the issues/constraints causing the delays in the implementation of said projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Adequately plan and study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the 20% LGDF;

Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the speedy implementation of the projects; and

Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.

We recommend that management:

Adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of law, rules, and regulations relative to the utilization of the LDRRM Fund, so that constituents could directly enjoy the benefits for which the fund was primarily established; and

Enforce strict control procedures in the disbursement of the fund to ensure that it is utilized only for its intended purpose to preclude the issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommend that management:

prioritize the creation of the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to focus on the issues and concerns on solid waste management;

address the issue of stray dogs that are scavenging through the garbage at collection points by the enactment of an ordinance and providing sanctions/penalties thereof; and

consider the procurement of additional dump truck and backhoe for the operation and maintenance of the Sanitary Landfill (SLF).

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12), and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”

b. The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12), and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”

b. The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

        We recommended management to direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   prepare and submit on time monthly bank reconciliation statements in  all
   bank accounts maintained by the municipality.

   Management’s Comments:

        The management is amenable with the Audit Team’s recommendation and
   assured the Team to comply the same.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Immediately correct the present system in handling PCF  and  ensure
        compliance with the NGAS for LGUs in maintaining the same under the
        imprest system;


     2. Use the PCF only for those  activities  requiring  items  of  small
        value equal to threshold when it is  inconvenient  to  pay  through
        check; and


     3. Adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in procuring  regular  expenses
        of the Municipality.


   Management’s Comments:

        The management agreed with the audit recommendations and  committed
   to implement them.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure  that  recorded  payables
        are duly supported with the required documents; have  notices  sent
        to identified payees and list thereon  will  be  furnished  to  the
        audit team for  evaluation  once  their  identity  and  claims  are
        definitely established; and


     2. Revert those payables aged more than two years which are determined
        to be without actual claimants.

   Management’s Comments:

        The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that most  of  the
   account payables represent outstanding checks that are long  outstanding.
   Further, Management affirmed to slowly revert the  balances  of  accounts
   payable.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort for the liquidation
        of Due from NGOs/POs account in order to avoid dormancy and closely
        monitor and regularly check the implementation of the projects;


     2. Exhaust all legal efforts to demand from the concerned NGOs/POs  to
        produce and submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents
        of   the   utilized   funds   transferred   to   their   respective
        organizations; and


     3. Prepare aging of dormant fund transfers to NGOs/POs on a  quarterly
        basis to  support  request  for  write-off  as  stipulated  in  COA
        Circular 2016-005.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to the audit  recommendations  and  informed  the
   team that in addition, they will secure an SB resolution to authorize the
   write-off of accounts.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Record fund transfers to barangay under the account  code  1-03-03-
        030 – Due from Local Government Units to ensure  proper  monitoring
        of fund releases and utilization thereof as  against  the  approved
        purpose; and


     2. Enforce liquidation of the fund transferred to different barangays.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to  the  audit  recommendations  and  assured  to
   comply with the same.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Cause refund of  the  claims  since  it  is  irregular  in  nature,
        otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit;


     2. Justify in writing as to the incurrence of  the  said  expenditures
        and stop using the Fund for purpose other than those stipulated  as
        list of priority projects aligned with the Utilization of  the  20%
        Development under prevailing guidelines; and


     3. Submit liquidation report on  disbursement  relative  to  “Araw  ng
        Bagumbayan”.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to  the  audit  recommendations  and  assured  to
   comply the same.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the MDRRMO to secure list of disaster volunteers  identified
        by the Municipality to be submitted to  the  Office  of  the  Civil
        Defense.  Likewise,  review  the   eligibility   requirements   for
        qualified individuals to be enlisted with the Office of  the  Civil
        Defense as accredited Community  Disaster  Volunteers,  and  secure
        legal basis/ existing specific guidelines issued by NDRRMC over the
        inclusion of  active  GSIS  members  as  members/volunteers  to  be
        provided with another accident insurance benefit package;


     2. Direct the concerned personnel/officer-in-  charge  to  submit  the
        policy contract on terms and conditions covered  by  the  insurance
        product to the Audit Team; and


     3. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the  correcting  entries
        showing the necessary adjustment pertaining thereto.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to the  audit  recommendations  and  assured  the
   Audit Team to comply the same.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to direct  the  LDRRMO  to  consider  the
   prior years’ unspent balance of DRRMF to be included in  the  preparation
   of LDRRMFIP and adopt the codes prescribed in the existing accounting and
   reporting guidelines.




   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to the audit recommendations and assured the Team
   to comply the same.

   for purposes not duly substantiated with legal authority.

        Management is amenable to the audit recommendations and promised to
   implement the same.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to direct the  Municipal  Accountant  and
   Municipal DRRM Officer to strictly observe the existing guidelines in the
   preparation and submission of  correct  Monthly  Report  of  Sources  and
   Allocation of DRRMF Fund.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management agreed to the  audit  recommendations  and  assured  the
   Audit Team to comply the same.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   cause and record the actual transfer of the unspent balance of  DRRMF  of
   prior years in the Trust Fund books to rectify the deficiencies noted  in
   the accounts involved.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management is amenable but assured the Audit Team  to  comply  with
   the abovementioned recommendation.

Recommendations:

        We recommended management to direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   stop granting cash advances to non-organic personnel to  avoid  delay  in
   the liquidation/loss or misappropriation of funds. Likewise, require  the
   settlement of cash advances which remained unliquidated as at year-end.


   Management’s Comments:

        Management  is  amenable  with  the  audit  recommendations.  Also,
   management also suggested that the clearance for DepEd  personnel  should
   go through the LGU to ensure that cash advances will be liquidated before
   the specific personnel will be cleared from DepEd.


        Further, liquidation reports  on  the  cash  advances  of  two  (2)
   district supervisors were submitted. The  two  (2)  supervisors  appealed
   that cash advances  be  continuously  granted  for  their  office  supply
   consumption since their office is not provided with Maintenance and Other
   Operating  Expenses  (MOOE)  budget.  Likewise,  they  are   asking   for
   consideration that travel expenses incurred from training  activities  be
   allowed in audit as they need funds to facilitate their participation  or
   attendance in similar activity to capacitate their teachers.

   COA’s Rejoinder:

        The audit team stands firm on its audit recommendations. Also,  the
   audit team emphasized that COA auditors are willing to hold  a  forum  to
   discuss with all the concerned personnel from LGUs within the Province of
   Davao Oriental the appropriate charges to SEF and other recurring  issues
   that require the LGU’s attention.

Recommendations:

        We recommended management to direct the  Budget  Office  to  ensure
   strict compliance in setting aside the minimum financial  requirement  or
   1% of Municipality’s IRA for the desired achievement  of  the  identified
   needs for the protection of children as determined by the Council.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management has acknowledged the deficiency  and  agreed  to  comply
   with the recommendation in the following year.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Provide all the information deemed necessary in the  evaluation  of
        the projects to be presented in  details  upon  submission  of  the
        report in order to  establish  reliability  of  the  accomplishment
        embodied therein; and


     2. Include those which are to  be  implemented  by  the  Municipality,
        whether infrastructure projects or other form  of  PPAs  so  as  to
        facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.

   Management’s Comments:

        The management informed the Audit Team that they will  comply  with
   all the recommendations mentioned.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Coordinate with  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  Office  (SB)  to  pass  a
        resolution  revisiting  and  formulating  a  doable  policies   and
        guidelines on the imposition of fines and  penalties,  taking  into
        consideration the  profit  sharing  ratio  with  the  participating
        barangays in the implementation of solid waste management; and


     2. Provide the adequate financial  support  and  number  of  technical
        manpower equipped with skills and  able  to  deliver  the  required
        services in support of Community  Education  and  Public  Awareness
        campaign and other duties and functions  deemed  necessary  in  the
        operation.

   Management’s Comments:

        The management is amenable with the recommendations  of  the  audit
   team and assured to comply the same.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  develop  or  adapt  available
        databases for the proper updating, monitoring and  determining  the
        total delinquent RPT per taxpayer;


     2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to expedite the
        collection of taxes due and collectible by sending  demand  letters
        to delinquent taxpayers giving  priority  to  delinquent  taxpayers
        having large balances;


     3. Create a committee which  is  responsible  in  the  conduct  of  an
        information dissemination drive regarding the importance of  paying
        taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment thereof; and


     4. Avail of the remedies provided for by law to enforce the strict and
        prompt collection of delinquent real property taxes.

   Management’s Comments:

        The management agreed to the recommendations of the Audit Team  and
   mentioned that they will  implement  the  computerization  of  their  RPT
   collection process in CY 2018. Further, it was also mentioned  that  they
   are exerting all the required  efforts  to  expedite  the  collection  of
   taxes. However, the Municipal Assessor’s records were  not  yet  updated.
   The Municipality has also sent field officers to issue demand letters and
   inform its constituents of their delinquent taxes.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the HRMO to formulate an employee manual  that  will  define
        the job description, rights and responsibilities, among others,  of
        all the employees within the Municipality;


     2. Issue a uniform and clear-cut policy on the  hiring  of  Job  Order
        workers to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and


     3. Reduce/limit the hiring of job order  workers  and  fill-up  vacant
        positions and evaluate  and  determine  the  appropriate  personnel
        complement of each office.

Management’s Comments:

        The management agreed to comply with the recommendations mentioned.
   Further, they have agreed to themselves that each  Department  Head  will
   submit to  HRMO  the  job  descriptions  and  responsibilities  of  their
   assigned employees. The HRMO will then consolidate  the  information  and
   will create a hiring policy based on it.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that GAD Focal Point  System  (Executive  Committee)
   comply with the GAD budget of at least 5%  of  the  Municipality’s  total
   appropriation.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management is amenable to the audit  recommendation  of  the  Audit
   Team and commits to adhere and implement them.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to establish a Monitoring and  Evaluation
   (M&E) Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD-responsive PPAs.


   Management’s Comments:

        Management is amenable to the audit  recommendation  of  the  Audit
   Team and commits to adhere and implement them.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to advise the GFPS to submit the required
   reports to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management is amenable to the audit  recommendation  of  the  Audit
   Team and commits to adhere and implement them.

Recommendations:

     1. Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE.  To
        ensure the success of  the  committee,  all  department  heads  are
        advised to instruct their members to cooperate in  the  conduct  of
        physical count;


     2. The  inventory  committee  shall  prepare  the  Physical  Inventory
        Reports to be submitted to the Property  Officer  for  verification
        and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards  to
        be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively;


     3.  All  fully  depreciated  PPE,  if  found  unserviceable  shall  be
        classified to Other Assets; and


     4. Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management is amenable to the audit  recommendation  of  the  Audit
   Team and commits to adhere and implement them.

Recommendation:

        We recommended management to direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   recognize monthly depreciation charges on all depreciable Property, Plant
   and Equipment in order to arrive at the correct net book value of PPE  as
   at year-end.

   Management’s Comments:

        Management informed the Audit  Team  that  the  previous  Municipal
   Treasurer had not completely turned over the records of some  PPE,  hence
   depreciation expenses of some items have not been computed.


   COA’s Rejoinder:

        The Audit Team stands  firmly  to  recognize  depreciation  on  all
   depreciable assets  to  present  the  account  fairly  in  the  financial
   statements.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following:

   Management’s Comments:

        The management informed the audit team that part of the balance  of
   Due from Officers and Employees were receivables  from  an  employee  who
   absconded.  Further,  those   unremitted   collections   represent   loan

   COA’s Rejoinder:

        The audit team stands firmly  on  the  recommendations  to  enforce

   vague accounts.

Recommendations:

        We recommended management to direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   maintain the required special accounts in the general fund.


   Management’s Comments:

        The management acknowledged that they have already been informed in

   subsidized and recorded under the  General  Fund  Proper.  Management  is

Recommendations:

        We recommended management to formulate  written  specific  policies

   Management’s Comments:

        The management is amenable with the audit recommendations and  will
   comply with the recommendations mentioned.

observations.managementAction: Accounting department is coping up with the timely submission.
observations.reason: This is due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management stop the practice of utilizing 20% Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created. We further recommend that preparation of the Annual Investment Plan should be compliant with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG Joint Circular Memorandum 2011-1 and ensure that utilization of the same is in accordance with the plan. With this, social, economic and environmental development would be achieved by the Municipality.
observations.reason: There were still disbursements that are not in line with the guidelines; included in 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit the hiring of job order to the actual needs of the concerned offices and to require the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) to coordinate with the Heads of Division/Office to prepare a well-defined job description of every JO stating therein the specific duties and responsibilities to ensure that the services rendered is necessary in the agency’s mandate and program objectives.
observations.reason: Included in 2016-2017 AAR. Refer to comments above.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the lacking vouchers. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before forwarding such to the Treasurer. With this, DVs as well as financial reports could be timely submitted to COA. Likewise, the legality and propriety of the transactions as well as the correctness of the entries could be evaluated and resolved on time.
observations.reason: This was due to voluminous transactions every month.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that requiring the Liquidating Officer and Municipal Treasurer to monitor closely the turned over of collections by collectors and prompt deposit of said collections by the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer to the Bank through strict enforcement of the provisions of Sections 60A, 60B and 60C of LTOM and Section 26 and 27 of COA Circular 92-382.
observations.managementAction: Constant and regular meeting with the collectors were conducted to remind them strict compliance and observance of COA rules and regulations on collections and timely remittance for prompt deposits of the amount collected.
observations.reason: Collectors held the sum collected and will only turn-over after constant monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Section should always use the BRS as guide in determining items needing adjustment and as basis in effecting prompt adjustment in the books as required in Section 52 of NGAS Manual, Volume 1 in order to reflect a fair presentation of cash and related accounts in the financial statements. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should monitor the submission of Bank Statements from the depository banks as well as prompt preparation of BRS to ensure accuracy of recording in the books. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant should communicate with the Head of the Depository Bank on various bank errors committed as appearing in the Bank Statement for appropriate correction of their records.
observations.managementAction: An index card for each employee was updated and such was used as basis for approval of processed disbursement voucher and before release of payment.
observations.reason: Due to the adverse effect that might affect the performance of office, there is a need for the approval of transactio ns immediatel y in the absence of the updated index card and sometimes even if there is an unliquidat ed balance.

observations.recommendation: Hasten the implementation of developmental projects embodied in the Municipal Annual Investment Plan within its time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the Municipality. Direct the Municipal Planning Development Council or the Municipal Project Engineer to exercise their monitoring function to immediately identify and address any problem(s) that tend to hamper/slow down development.
observations.managementAction: Review the annual investment program and hasten the full implementation of development projects.
observations.reason: Continuing appropriations for development projects were implemented the following year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that prompt submission of all financial reports of the Municipality be observed as prescribed in Sections 70, 72, 74 and 100 of PD 1445 and also in Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-006 in order to allow timely evaluation, analysis, and audit of accounts and transactions. Also, the Management was reminded the Sec. 122 of PD 1445 that non-submission of pertinent reports may result to the following: “(2) Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission. (3) No appropriation authorized in the General Appropriations Act shall be available to pay the salary or any official or employee who violates the provisions of this section, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee.”
observations.managementAction: Submission of monthly reports promptly is given importance and priority but other documents need to be received to complete the reports. Coordination with other offices concerned was done to fast track the recording of accounts.
observations.reason: Due to various programs implemented, the office lacks plantilla personnel to record/compile and prepare necessary documents to immediately complete the reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare Lapsing Schedule showing the computation of the accumulated depreciation of all the Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts to ensure that all properties are provided with the appropriate allowance for depreciation. Prepare and update postings of the Property Ledger Cards and fill in all the necessary data most especially the date of acquisition and estimated useful life to facilitate preparation of the said schedule. Be guided by COA Circular 2003-007 for the revised estimated life of PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel reconcile the accounts after payment against the appropriation and immediately maintain the property ledger card and subsidiary ledger. However, prior to the implementation of the Local Government Code of 1991, the ledger cards were maintained by other office and were not turned over to the office of the accountant.
observations.reason: Computatio n of accumulate d depreciati on on acquired before 1993 can no longer be obtained. Nevertheless, acquisition of PPE from 1993 to present had an updated computatio n of accumulate d depreciati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should clearly monitor the use of government motor vehicles which should be limited to official purposes only through the proper accomplishment of trip tickets on a daily basis. Trip tickets and pertinent monthly reports should be filled in appropriately taking into consideration the following: a) Signature and name of passengers b) Odometer/ speedometer reading at the start and end of the trip c) Place and Purpose of the trip d) Quantity purchased during the trip e) Beginning and ending balance of fuel in tank per trip f) Name and type of vehicle and the plate number. Moreover, require the immediate repair of defective odometer/speedometer. The driver of the motor vehicle concerned shall be held responsible for failure to report defective ones as well as the official who authorizes the said transaction. Follow strictly the provisions of COA Circular 77-61. Refrain from refuelling gasoline to private vehicles otherwise it would be a proper charge to the driver and Officer who authorizes the said transaction.
observations.managementAction: The observation was discussed to all concerned personnel.
observations.reason: Refueling to private vehicles cannot be avoided sometimes since such were used in the operation. The management lacks vehicles to immediately respond to transport goods and services to different barangays. But the management discourage d such refueling most often.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the Motor pool in- charge should comply strictly with the provisions of Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. A preliminary inspection on the motor vehicle subjected for repair should be conducted to determine its physical condition, the extent of damage and repair needed and subsequently conduct post inspection after the repair to ensure that the repair has been undertaken in accordance with the plan and scope of work. Require the submission of the required reports as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: This was discussed with the GSO and Municipal Engineer. They discussed the compliance with the preliminary inspection on the motor vehicles subjected for repair and subsequently conduct a post inspection.
observations.reason: Emergency repairs were done due to the following: Not to cause delay in the implementa tion of government projects Emergency situation

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer shall strengthen control measures in the handling of cash by collecting and liquidating Officers. Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 83, Vlll.3 of LTOM, COA Circular 92-125A dated March 1992, Section 70 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual in order not to expose government funds to risk of misappropriation or misuse. We recommend reshuffle of personnel in the Treasury as often as necessary.
observations.reason: The collection s that were not deposited at year end pertain to late payments of Real Property Taxes received by field collectors. The shortage of the liquidating officer during the inventory made of all unprocessed vouchers and unclaimed checks found in his custody. Analysis and further verification on shall be made by her office in order to effect adjustments in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency should stop the setting up of Accounts Payable to purchases with no actual deliveries made and not duly supported with valid contract. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to verify the status of long outstanding accounts payable.  If indeed, the claimants still exists, a written notice shall be sent to the respective payees informing them of their existing claims otherwise the remaining accounts shall be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund as required.
observations.reason: Payables pertain to accounts that were left behind by the late Municipal Treasurer, during the inventory made of all unprocessed vouchers and unclaimed checks found in his custody. Analysis and further verificati on shall be made by her office in order to effect adjustments in the books.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least every quarter as a sound cash management practice and handling, in order to generate reliable and timely financial statements. The personnel handling the receipt and issuance of accountable forms should prepare the monthly or quarterly RAAF in compliance with existing regulations, and the commitment to submit timely reports to governing authorities.
observations.reason: The reconcilia tion of records is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Require the focal person for the said program or the program coordinator to submit written explanation for the deviation of the program implementation, concerning releases of payments to beneficiaries. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to explain the gap of more than two (2) months since the fund was received until the actual payment to beneficiaries. Require the Disbursing Officer to submit notarized certification of actual date and amount released to beneficiaries per Payroll.
observations.reason: The beneficiaries were located in order for them to affixed signature and prepare for notarizati on.

observations.recommendation: Always demand an Official Receipt for every check released in payment of every government transaction to avoid suspension or disallowance of the same. observations.reason: The matter is understudy for in preparatio n for some legal action by the LGU in the event that the BIR personnel won’t issue OR for remittances after final demand.

observations.recommendation: Stop the granting of cash advance to any elected official except for official traveling expenses pursuant to the above-mentioned provision. observations.reason: Some of the unliquidated cash advances are from the Intelligence Fund of which the documents were submitted but not credited awaiting for the Credit Notice. Lack of monitoring on long outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Cause the observance of the principle laid down in the Local Government Code in formulating sound financial plans that shall operationalize development plans. observations.recommendation:
Based on the above observations, we recommended the Municipal General Services Officer to perform periodic reconciliation of property records maintained to ensure the veracity of the reported amounts of PPE in the financial statements.

Also, please conduct physical count on the twenty-three PPE accounts.

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.
...
For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations: Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12), and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor. Xxx”

b. The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”

Comply the provisions of Budget Circulars Nos. 2004-05A and 2007-3. observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

       We recommended that income generating fund should only be  released
   to accredited NGO/POs and that liquidation report be required in order to
   determine the reliability of its utilization.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that Management should ensure that claims should  be
   supported with complete documentation in order to prevent suspensions and
   disallowances in audit.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should comply with the
   documentary requirements provided under Sec. 3 of COA Circular No.  2012-
   001 to ensure the propriety and validity of the transaction and to record
   fund transfers to barangay under the account code 1-03-03-030 – Due  from
   Local Government Units to ensure proper monitoring of fund releases.


        We  further  recommended  to  enforce  liquidation  of   the   fund
   transferred to these barangays to ensure that this was utilized  for the
   intended projects.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that  preparation  of  the  Annual  Investment  Plan
   should be in compliance with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG  Joint
   Circular Memorandum  2011-1  and  ensure  that  utilization  of  the  20%
   Development Fund should be in accordance with the plan.
        We  also  recommended  to  stop  the  practice  of  utilizing   20%
   Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created.


        We further recommended to direct the Municipal Development  Council
   to provide detailed descriptions and/or project designs for all the  PPAs
   in the AIP to determine if such were attributed for development purposes.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the following courses of action:

   a. Action  Plan  should  be  prepared  indicating  therein  the  specific
      programs and activities to be funded and implemented out of this fund.
   b.  Consider  increasing  the  appropriation  of  the  MDRRMO   for   the
      administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office and ensure charges to  the
      LDRRM Fund are limited only to programs and activities in  support  of
      disaster risk reduction and management.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that management should avoid charging  expenses  not
   within the priority expenses as enumerated under DECS, DBM and DILG Joint
   Circular No. 01 and to advise the Local School Board to effectively  plan
   for the specific programs, projects and activities which strictly  adhere
   to the abovementioned provisions.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the Management should exert efforts to  collect
   these collectibles so that whatever amount collected can be used for  the
   operation of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should  determine
   the collectivity of these accounts so that proper  adjustments  could  be
   made in order not to overstate the receivable balances as at year-end.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to discontinue the granting of honoraria pursuant to the cited provisions of laws to avoid further suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: They have stopped paying honoraria to some of these personnel.
observations.reason: Some personnel were still granted since they are performing additional tasks on top of their job description.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management to only grant CNA to its employees if there is a legal source of fund and have accomplished an average of 70% of PPAs to avoid suspensions and disallowance.

We recommended the management strictly adhere the rate prescribed under Sec. 5.3 of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit. Likewise, we enforced them to submit a proof for a successfully completed procurement project monthly to enable us to determine the correct amount of honoraria to be received by each concerned personnel.
We recommended the management to stop granting uniform allowance to employees who do not have an employee-employer relationship to avoid suspensions and disallowance.

We recommended that management review and conduct proper study of the existing approved plantilla positions of each Office to determine the actual personnel requirements needed. Also, we recommended that Management limit the hiring of job order to the actual needs of the concerned offices and to require the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) to coordinate with the Heads of Division/Office to prepare a well-defined job description of every JO stating therein the specific duties and responsibilities to ensure that the services rendered is necessary in the agency’s mandate and program objectives. Likewise, to avoid suspension in audit, the Management is required to submit the supporting documents to COA. Furthermore, hiring a number of job order workers do not speak well on the economic and efficient handling of the Municipality’s resources which could have been used in other programs more responsive to the needs of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management is still on the process of assessing the vacant positions.
observations.reason: Accordingly, some offices necessitates to hire job orders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management and its human resource office to hire/fill up the mandatory vacant positions as mandated under Sec. 4.2 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 thus, creating a span of control and chain of command in the implementation of DRRM programs.

Establishing mandatory positions in the MDRRMO in every LGU is one of the basic elements, among others, in the aspect of disaster preparedness. This gives the primary responsibility to a specific office in carrying out or implementing various disaster risk reduction and management plans/programs of the municipality.

We recommended that the 30% QRF shall only be utilized for relief and recovery program/activities and only when there is a declared state of calamity.

We further recommended that Management exercise prudence and diligence in utilizing the calamity fund to prevent losses to government funds.

We recommended that management limit their expenditures to the appropriations during the year to avoid incurrence of deficiency in appropriations which is a clear indication of misapplication or misuse of appropriated funds. In addition, projects identified during the year might not be fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare Lapsing Schedule showing the computation of the accumulated depreciation of all the Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts to ensure that all properties are provided with the appropriate allowance for depreciation.

Prepare and update postings of the Property Ledger Cards and fill in all the necessary data most especially the date of acquisition and estimated useful life to facilitate preparation of the said schedule.

Be guided by COA Circular 2003-007 for the revised estimated life of PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel reconcile the accounts after payment against the appropriation and immediately maintain the property ledger and subsidiary ledger. However, prior to the implementation of the Local Government Code of 1991, the ledger cards were maintained by other office and were not turned over to the office of the accountant.
observations.reason: Computation of accumulated depreciation acquired before 1993 can no longer be obtained. Nevertheless, acquisition of PPE from 1993 to present had an updated computation of accumulated depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management should clearly monitor the use of government motor vehicles which should be limited to official purposes only through the proper accomplishment of trip tickets on a daily basis. Trip tickets and pertinent monthly reports should be filled in appropriately taking into consideration the following:

a) Signature and name of passengers
b) Odometer/speedometer reading at the start and end of the trip
c) Place and Purpose of the trip
d) Quantity purchased during the trip
e) Beginning and ending balance of fuel in tank per trip
f) Name and type of vehicle and the plate number

Moreover, require the immediate repair of defective odometer/speedometer. The driver of the motor vehicle concerned shall be held responsible for failure to report defective ones as well as the official who neglects to undertake the repair.

Follow strictly the provisions of COA Circular 77-61. Refrain from refuelling gasoline to private vehicles otherwise it would be a proper charge to the driver and Officer who authorizes the said transaction.
observations.managementAction: The observation was discussed to all concerned personnel.
observations.reason: Refueling to private vehicles cannot be avoided sometimes since such were used in the operation. The management lacks vehicles to immediately respond to different barangays. But the management discouraged such refueling most often.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the Motor pool in-charge should comply strictly with the provisions of Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. A preliminary inspection on the motor vehicle subjected for repair should be conducted to determine its physical condition, the extent of damage and repair needed and subsequently conduct post inspection after the repair to ensure that the repair has been undertaken in accordance with the plan and scope of work.

Require the submission of the required reports as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: This was discussed with the GSO and Municipal Engineer. They discussed the compliance with the preliminary inspection on the motor vehicles subjected for repair and subsequently conduct s post inspection.
observations.reason: Emergency repairs were done due to the following: Not to cause delay in the implementati on of government projects Emergency situation

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer shall strengthen control measures in the handling of cash by collecting and liquidating Officers. Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 83, Vlll.3 of LTOM, COA Circular 92-125A dated March 1992, Section 70 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual in order not to expose government funds to risk of misappropriation or misuse. We recommend reshuffle of personnel in the Treasury as often as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The agency is coping up with the recommendation of COA in the preparation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report.
observations.reason: The collections that were not deposited at year end pertain to late payments of Real Property Taxes received by field collectors. The shortage of the liquidating officer during the count was immediately restored within the day as the amount was not properly accounted during the cash count.

observations.recommendation: The agency should stop the setting up of Accounts Payable to purchases with no actual deliveries made and not duly supported with valid contract. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to verify the status of long outstanding accounts payable. If indeed, the claimants still exists, a written notice shall be sent to the respective payees informing them of their existing claims otherwise the remaining accounts shall be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund as required.
observations.managementAction: The agency suggests that COA should introduce more account codes to address the needs of users.
observations.reason: Payables pertain to accounts that were left behind by the late Municipal Treasurer, during the inventory made of all unprocessed vouchers and unclaimed checks found in his custody. Analysis and further verification shall be made by her office in order to effect adjustments in the books.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least every quarter as a sound cash management practice and handling, in order to generate reliable and timely financial statements.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of records is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
Require the focal person for the said program or the program coordinator to submit written explanation for the deviation of the program concerning releases of payments to beneficiaries.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to explain the gap of more than two (2) months since the fund was received until the actual payment to beneficiaries.

Require the Disbursing Officer to submit notarized certification of actual date and amount released to beneficiaries per Payroll.
observations.reason: The beneficiaries were located in order for them to affixed signature and prepare for notarization.

observations.recommendation:
Always demand an Official Receipt for every check released in payment of every government transaction to avoid suspension or disallowance of the same.

Issuance of original copy of Revenue Official Receipt (ROR) in acknowledgment of the receipt of payment of the above transactions should be demanded by the Municipal Treasurer from the concerned BIR Official, then attached to paid DVs accordingly and properly.
observations.reason: The matter is under study for preparation for some legal action by the LGU with the BIR in the event that the BIR personnel won’t issue OR for remittances after final demand.

observations.recommendation:
Stop the granting of cash advance to any elected official except for official traveling expenses pursuant to the above-mentioned provision. Also, the agency should designate a Disbursing Officer who is a permanently appointed official of the municipality for cash advances with special purpose such as those used for fiesta and other activities.

Submit to this Office copies of JEVs with the complete attached original copies of liquidation report and supporting documents as valid credits to the Cash Disbursing Officers (103) account.
observations.reason: Some of the unliquidated cash advances are from the Intelligence Fund of which the documents were submitted but not credited awaiting for the Credit Notice. Lack of monitoring on long outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units on the submission of certified list of RPT/SET collectibles which should be the basis of recording receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office requested the Assessor’s Office to facilitate copy of tax roll per barangay to be able to come up with the required provision and is now on process of encoding individual tax roll per barangay.

observations.recommendation:
The Head of the Agency and the Officers listed above should refund the excess claim over authorized amount per SB Resolution No. 2006-121, and in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 85-55A dated Sept. 8, 1985.

The said SB Resolutions should be reviewed and evaluated.

The manner of payment must also be deliberated as to its applicability and fairness among all the Heads of Offices, and the procurement procedures should be in accordance with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Effective immediately after the exit conference last March 18, 2011, the Agency implemented the regulation on the claims of cellphone cards.

All claims for communication expenses are done through payroll to have a fair and equal claim as recommended in the AAR of 2010.

The said allowance has been reviewed and a new guidelines was resolved to be implemented. There was failure to refund the excess claims as previously recommended.
observations.reason: Review of said allowance has been reviewed and a new guidelines was resolved to be implemented. There was failure to refund the excess claims as previously recommended.

observations.recommendation:
The drivers and concerned officers assigned with motor vehicle should accomplish/fill up properly the Driver’s Trip Tickets, and supply the data or required information in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61, or the Manual for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

The Monthly Fuel Consumption Report should be submitted regularly for the evaluation of the correctness of expenses incurred by the Municipality in a given period.
observations.managementAction: The agency is on strict implementation following the recommendation of COA in the preparation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report.
observations.reason: Claims for fuel still lack the necessary supporting documents such as Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Fuel Consumption Report, etc.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to identify the finished projects and prepare the certificate of completed projects to serve as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in making adjusting entries through a JEV transferring the completed projects from Construction In Progress to Public Infrastructure accounts.

The assets should be recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructures.
observations.managementAction: Based on the Accomplishment Report of completed projects submitted by the Office of Municipal Engineer, the accounting office made corresponding adjustment thru journal entry voucher transferring construction in process account to respective Public Infrastructure account. The Public Infrastructure accounts used for general public were closed to the Government Equity account while maintaining Registry for each account.
observations.reason: Validation/verification was not done because COA-copies/files of JEVs were not yet submitted to the Audit Team despite verbal & written demands for submission of the same.

observations.recommendation:
Cause the observance of the principle laid down in the Local Government Code in formulating sound financial plans that shall operationalize development plans.

Provide for an equitable allocation of resources and ensure that the local budgets shall be based on functions, activities and projects, in terms of expected results.

Avoid allocating lump sum appropriation where actual activities and expected outputs have yet to be identified during the year by its implementers.

Require the conduct of budget hearing as set forth under the Local government Code to determine which activities of the different departments or offices need to be prioritized.
observations.managementAction: The agency suggests that COA should introduce more account codes to address the needs of users.
observations.reason: The charges to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses (969) have been reduced in the current year. However, the same is still subject to post audit and verification. There is still a need for proper coordination between Accounting and Budget Office.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate processing of the documents necessary for the registration of the land under the Torrens title System as required under Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 29, 1992 pertaining to Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management by the Local Government in order to establish ownership and to safeguard the properties from unlawful occupants and to avoid unwarranted expenses in the future.
observations.managementAction: The management had started the titling of some of the purchased lots during the year. However, for the lots situated at Barangays Ilangay, Don Mariano Marcos and the lot for cemetery expansion in Poblacion these are still in process.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, they have to settle first the land conflict and technical survey problems on these concerned lots before working on the lot titling.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the equipment previously accounted for as expenses to asset account in accordance with the provisions of the NGAS for LGUs. Likewise, establish the property accountability of the officials personally having custody or possession of government property thru the issuance of appropriate Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments of some accounts were made in 2012.
observations.reason: Similar procurement in 2011 and 2012 were made, but at the Management tried to correct and adjust records towards year end of 2013.
